prompt: The equals() call always returns false even when comparing two Point objects with identical coordinates. What is the bug?
choices: p1 and p2 must be the same variable for equals() to return true
Point does not override equals() — it inherits Object.equals() which compares references, not field values
int fields cannot be compared with equals(); use == directly on x and y
equals() always returns false for objects created with new
explanation: Object.equals() compares references by default: p1.equals(p2) is equivalent to p1 == p2, which is false because p1 and p2 are distinct objects. To compare by content, Point must override equals(): @Override public boolean equals(Object o) { if (!(o instanceof Point p)) return false; return x == p.x && y == p.y; } It's also good practice to override hashCode() consistently.
